results.rendered_html: ūdhas m. n. (in Veda also) ūdhan, ūdhar (see  Whitney's Gr. 430 b ; in classical Sanskṛt the stem ūdhan appears only in the fem. of an adj. compound, e.g. kuṇḍoDnI &c.) (√vah Comm. on  Uṇ. iv, 192 ; √ud,  T. ) the udder of any female, breast, bosom,  RV. ;  AV. ;  ŚBr. ;  MBh.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
ūdhas n.
1 An udder (changed to ūdhan in Bah. comp.); bhuvaṃ koṣṇena kuṃḍodhnī medhyenāvabhṛthādapi  R. 1. 84. 
2 A covered secret place to which only friends are admitted.
3 The bosom.
4 The breast of a mother.
